leagueBriefing: The TGL finals are set after a night of high-stakes drama at the SoFi Center, where the 'Bad News Bears' of Jupiter Links GC completed a stunning worst-to-first turnaround and Los Angeles Golf Club used a tactical masterclass to dethrone the defending champions.

games.headline: Tiger's Jupiter Links completes underdog story to reach TGL final
games.brief: In a true David vs. Goliath matchup, Jupiter Links GC—the team that finished dead last in the inaugural season—stunned Rory McIlroy's star-studded Boston Common 9-5. Max Homa provided the highlight of the night, sweeping his singles holes against McIlroy and sealing the victory with a clutch par-saver on the 13th before breaking out Steph Curry's iconic 'night night' celebration to send Boston home.
bullets: Max Homa delivered the knockout blow, winning both of his singles holes against world No. 2 Rory McIlroy.
Alternate Akshay Bhatia continued his hot streak, having shot par or better in 20 straight PGA Tour rounds since February.
Tiger Woods, though sidelined by injury, praised the team's 'Bad News Bears' mentality and chemistry as the key to their turnaround.
The match included a tense moment where McIlroy reportedly threw Tom Kim's putter after a refused concession.
Jupiter Links advances to the final after finishing the previous season in the league basement.
games.context: Jupiter Links GC completes a remarkable worst-to-first journey to compete for the TGL championship.

games.headline: Fleetwood's tactical 'Hammer' sends Los Angeles to TGL finals
games.brief: Los Angeles Golf Club punched their ticket to the finals by dethroning the defending champion Atlanta Drive in a 6-4 thriller defined by strategy. Tommy Fleetwood turned the tide on the 14th hole with a perfectly timed 'Hammer' throw that forced Atlanta into a high-pressure mistake. Justin Rose provided the veteran stability, draining a 24-foot birdie to spark the comeback and another at the 14th to take the lead for good.
bullets: Tommy Fleetwood used the league's 'Hammer' mechanic on the 14th hole to pressure Atlanta into a pivotal scoring error.
Justin Rose kickstarted the LA comeback with a massive 24-foot birdie putt and later secured the lead at the 14th.
Atlanta's Billy Horschel declined the hammer on the 14th, a decision that ultimately led to the team falling behind 6-4.
Defending champions Atlanta Drive are officially eliminated from title contention.
Rose described the team's late-match strategy as 'genius' despite admitting initial confusion over the Hammer deployment.
games.context: The defending champions have been dethroned, ensuring a new winner will be crowned in the 2026 TGL season.
